data.body: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via joe-debug): the mkdir leg of the write lock (.ticket-write.lock.d) had no stale reclamation — unlike the kernel-reclaimed fcntl leg — so a dir orphaned by a process that died mid-commit blocked every subsequent write the full ~60s budget then failed (proven: acquire with a stale dir present times out; a write hangs then errors). FIX (branch worktree-civil-marlin-flare, commit 80fef546, PR #26): acquire() stamps the owning host:pid inside the lock dir and _acquire_mkdir reclaims ONLY a lock whose stamp proves a DEAD process on THIS host — live owner / ownerless / foreign-host are never reclaimed (3 safety guards; mutation-checked). Race-safe because the fcntl leg (acquired first) serializes Python acquirers. release() removes the stamp before rmdir. Reads were already made immune in slim-fetch-ledge (commit 9943f1e1); this covers writes. Pending PR merge before close.
